fallbackRules.description: When exact weight not available, map to closest

500-to-400.message: Using Regular for labels and UI

goldenRules: Use weight for emphasis, not size
forbidden: More than 4 sizes in one view

spacing.philosophy: Parsed, not guessed. Spacing values must be enforced programmatically — never eyeballed.

layers.description: One scale, two layers. Modern design systems don't choose between 4pt or 8pt — they assign responsibility.

precision.name: 4pt detail layer
precision.role: Icons, typography baselines, and internal component spacing
precision.use: Fine-grained adjustments inside components where 8pt is too coarse

rhythm.name: 8pt rhythm layer
rhythm.role: Layout spacing, padding, margins, and component sizing
rhythm.use: Creates consistent visual rhythm at the layout level

baselineGrid.purpose: Typography must snap to the 8pt baseline grid for vertical rhythm
baselineGrid.rule: Line-heights should be multiples of 8px so text aligns across columns and components
baselineGrid.antiPattern: Type that doesn't snap to the baseline grid looks unanchored and breaks vertical rhythm

componentPatterns.buttonGroup: flex gap-2 (8px compact) or gap-3 (12px standard)

forbidden: Off-grid values: 5px, 7px, 18px, 29px — random values confuse users
Typography that doesn't snap to 8pt baseline grid

randomValues.description: Random values confuse users — spacing like 5px, 7px, 18px, 29px creates visual noise
randomValues.fix: Use only values from the scale: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 40, 48

typographyOffGrid.description: Typography that doesn't snap to the baseline grid breaks vertical rhythm
typographyOffGrid.fix: Set line-heights to multiples of 8px (16px, 24px, 32px) so baselines align

guessingSpacing.description: Eyeballing spacing values leads to inconsistency across components
guessingSpacing.fix: Parse and validate spacing values against the scale — never guess

principles.corePhilosophy: White space is not empty — it is an active design tool that creates structure, hierarchy, focus, rhythm, and comfort. Treating space as 'unused' leads to clutter, cognitive overload, and loss of hierarchy.
rules: Space defines grouping and separates content blocks
If everything is filled, nothing stands out
Spacing is the primary signal of hierarchy — larger gaps between sections than within sections
Readability depends more on space than on font choice
Vertical spacing controls pace, comfort, and flow
One clear intent per component — more content does not equal more value
Components inherit spacing rules from the system, not invent their own
White space must scale with screen size
principles.designOrder: Good layouts are built with space first — content second

fillingEveryGap.description: Filling every available gap with content reduces scannability, makes layouts harder to parse, and forces users to work harder to understand structure
symptoms: Content feels overwhelming on first glance
Users cannot identify primary actions or content
fillingEveryGap.rule: If everything is filled, nothing stands out
fillingEveryGap.fix: Use white space to define grouping, separate content blocks, and clarify intent — leave intentional gaps

sectionsBleeding.description: Sections visually bleed into each other when vertical gaps between sections are not significantly larger than gaps within sections
symptoms: Users cannot tell where one section ends and another begins
sectionsBleeding.rule: Spacing is the primary signal of hierarchy
sectionsBleeding.fix: Use section-level tokens (gap-12/gap-16, 48-64px) between sections and smaller tokens (gap-4/gap-6, 16-24px) within sections — the ratio should be at least 2:1

crowdingTextBlocks.description: Tight paragraphs and minimal line/section spacing kills readability and increases cognitive load, making long-form content exhausting to read
symptoms: Users skip content because it looks effortful
crowdingTextBlocks.rule: Readability depends more on space than on font choice
crowdingTextBlocks.fix: Increase spacing between paragraphs more than between lines — use mb-4 to mb-6 between paragraphs, leading-relaxed for long-form content

overpackingComponents.description: Stuffing cards and components with too many elements (buttons, tags, metadata, badges) increases cognitive load and blurs the component's primary purpose
symptoms: Cards have more than 5-6 distinct elements
Primary action is hard to identify
Component tries to serve multiple purposes
overpackingComponents.rule: More content does not equal more value — one clear intent per component
overpackingComponents.fix: Remove non-essential elements, use progressive disclosure (expand/detail views), limit cards to: title + description + 1 primary action + 1-2 metadata items

inconsistentComponentSpacing.description: Each component inventing its own internal spacing logic makes the system feel fragmented and harder to maintain at scale
inconsistentComponentSpacing.rule: Components inherit spacing rules from the system — spacing tokens are reused, not improvised
inconsistentComponentSpacing.fix: Define spacing tokens per component type: cards use p-6, forms use space-y-4, button groups use gap-2 or gap-3 — never ad-hoc values

ignoringVerticalRhythm.description: Treating spacing as purely horizontal while ignoring vertical rhythm makes the interface flow feel erratic and uncomfortable
ignoringVerticalRhythm.rule: Vertical spacing controls pace, comfort, and flow
ignoringVerticalRhythm.fix: Establish predictable Y-axis spacing patterns — repeat the same vertical rhythm across sections using the 8pt rhythm layer

responsiveSpacing.philosophy: White space must scale with screen size — the same spacing values on mobile and desktop create different perceptual experiences
responsiveSpacing.rule: Desktop spacing feels cramped at mobile values; mobile spacing feels oversized at desktop values
responsiveSpacing.tailwindPattern: p-4 md:p-5 lg:p-6 for progressive spacing increase
forbidden: Using identical spacing at all breakpoints
Only adjusting layout (stack-to-row) without adjusting spacing

metaLessons.spacingOverColor: Spacing communicates hierarchy more clearly than color — use spacing to express structure before reaching for color differentiation
metaLessons.randomSpacingDestroysTrust: Random spacing makes interfaces feel unpolished and accidental, reducing user trust even when functionality is correct
metaLessons.rhythmOverDensity: Rhythm (predictable, repeating spacing patterns) is more important than density (fitting more content) — a comfortable layout outperforms a packed one
metaLessons.spaceFirst: Good layouts are built with space first, content second — define the spatial structure before placing elements

commonViolations.issue: Using Tailwind standard blue text colors
Using Tailwind standard red text colors
Using Tailwind standard green text colors
Using Tailwind standard gray text colors

commonViolations.fix: Use design system tokens (bg-primary-*, text-neutral-*, etc.)
Use design system tokens with opacity modifiers (bg-primary-500/50)

commonViolations.issue: Arbitrary text size breaks typography scale
commonViolations.fix: Use text-xs, text-sm, text-base, text-lg, text-xl, text-2xl

commonViolations.issue: Arbitrary text size in rem breaks typography scale
commonViolations.fix: Use text-xs, text-sm, text-base, text-lg, text-xl, text-2xl
Use Tailwind spacing scale (gap-1 to gap-24, p-1 to p-24)

commonViolations.issue: Arbitrary space utility breaks 4px grid system
commonViolations.fix: Use space-x-1 to space-x-24 or space-y-1 to space-y-24

commonViolations.issue: Arbitrary width - consider if semantic width fits
commonViolations.fix: Use w-full, w-auto, or Tailwind width scale when possible

commonViolations.issue: Arbitrary height - consider if semantic height fits
commonViolations.fix: Use h-full, h-auto, h-screen, or Tailwind height scale when possible
Fix cascade/specificity at source, use Tailwind layers
Use Tailwind classes or CSS variables
Use rounded-sm, rounded, rounded-md, rounded-lg, rounded-xl, rounded-full

commonViolations.issue: Arbitrary z-index can cause stacking issues
commonViolations.fix: Use z-0, z-10, z-20, z-30, z-40, z-50 for predictable layering

accessibilityViolations.fix: Add alt="description" for informative images or alt="" for decorative
Add aria-label for meaningful icons or aria-hidden="true" for decorative
Use <button> for actions or <a> for navigation, or add role="button" tabIndex={0}

accessibilityViolations.issue: Clickable div is not keyboard accessible
accessibilityViolations.fix: Use <button> instead, or add role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown handler

accessibilityViolations.issue: Clickable span is not keyboard accessible
accessibilityViolations.fix: Use <button> instead, or add role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown handler

accessibilityViolations.issue: Negative tabIndex removes element from tab order
accessibilityViolations.fix: Only use tabIndex={-1} for programmatically focused elements

accessibilityViolations.issue: Positive tabIndex disrupts natural tab order
accessibilityViolations.fix: Use tabIndex={0} or restructure DOM for natural order

accessibilityViolations.issue: autoFocus can disorient screen reader users
accessibilityViolations.fix: Use sparingly, only for modals/dialogs where focus management is needed

accessibilityViolations.issue: Input without id may lack label association
accessibilityViolations.fix: Add id and associate with <label htmlFor="id">

accessibilityViolations.issue: Icon-only button may lack accessible name
accessibilityViolations.fix: Add aria-label="description" or visually hidden text

accessibilityViolations.issue: Placeholder is not a substitute for labels
accessibilityViolations.fix: Add visible <label> or aria-label for accessibility

accessibilityViolations.issue: Color alone used to convey meaning
accessibilityViolations.fix: Add icons, text, or patterns alongside color for colorblind users
Use <button> for actions or add href for navigation
Use <button> for actions, or provide meaningful href

requiredPatterns.fix: Add type="button" to prevent form submission, or type="submit" for forms

requiredPatterns.issue: Component with variants should use cn() for class merging
requiredPatterns.fix: Use cn() from lib/utils for proper Tailwind class merging

requiredPatterns.issue: Reusable component should support ref forwarding
requiredPatterns.fix: Wrap component with React.forwardRef for ref support
